pi_items.source: Jitsi Meet official documentation and GitHub repo; confirmed as real config option with default value false
Jitsi Meet official documentation; confirmed as real config option with default value true
Jitsi Meet official documentation; confirmed as real config option with default value false

pi_items.metadata_value: disableAudioLevels and enableNoisyMicDetection are CPU-intensive features
pi_items.verified_value: disableAudioLevels false causes significant CPU usage (confirmed by Jitsi community reports); enableNoisyMicDetection true runs VADNoiseDetection service consuming CPU resources
pi_items.source: Jitsi Community Forum discussions and GitHub issues #6920, #13544

pi_items.metadata_value: startAudioOnly: true enforces audio-only mode for low-power devices
pi_items.verified_value: startAudioOnly when set to true starts the conference in audio-only mode with no video received or sent, reducing bandwidth and client CPU usage

privileged_info_summary: This task involves optimizing a Jitsi Meet configuration file at /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js by setting three real configuration parameters: disableAudioLevels to true (disables CPU-intensive audio level measurement), enableNoisyMicDetection to false (disables noisy microphone detection), and startAudioOnly to true (starts conferences in audio-only mode). All three parameters are legitimate Jitsi Meet configuration options with documented CPU/bandwidth optimization benefits. The task uses a synthetic Jitsi Meet Docker environment where the initial config file is created with unoptimized values (disableAudioLevels: false, enableNoisyMicDetection: true, startAudioOnly: false) that must be changed by editing the file. The verifier script checks for these three settings using regex pattern matching on the config file content.
